source: NIST (U.S. Government work, public domain) — https://www.nist.gov/privacy-framework

controls.title: Systems/products/services that process data are inventoried
controls.description: Systems/products/services that process data are inventoried

controls.title: Owners or operators (e.g., the organization or third parties such as service providers, partners, customers, and developers) and their roles with respect to the systems/products/services and components (e.g., internal or external) that process data are inventoried
controls.description: Owners or operators (e.g., the organization or third parties such as service providers, partners, customers, and developers) and their roles with respect to the systems/products/services and components (e.g., internal or external) that process data are inventoried

controls.title: Categories of individuals (e.g., customers, employees or prospective employees, consumers) whose data are being processed are inventoried
controls.description: Categories of individuals (e.g., customers, employees or prospective employees, consumers) whose data are being processed are inventoried

controls.title: Data actions of the systems/products/services are inventoried
controls.description: Data actions of the systems/products/services are inventoried

controls.title: The purposes for the data actions are inventoried
controls.description: The purposes for the data actions are inventoried

controls.title: Data elements within the data actions are inventoried
controls.description: Data elements within the data actions are inventoried

controls.title: The data processing environment is identified (e.g., geographic location, internal, cloud, third parties)
controls.description: The data processing environment is identified (e.g., geographic location, internal, cloud, third parties)

controls.title: Data processing is mapped, illustrating the data actions and associated data elements for systems/products/services, including components; roles of the component owners/operators; and interactions of individuals or third parties with the systems/products/services
controls.description: Data processing is mapped, illustrating the data actions and associated data elements for systems/products/services, including components; roles of the component owners/operators; and interactions of individuals or third parties with the systems/products/services

controls.title: The organization's role(s) in the data processing ecosystem are identified and communicated
controls.description: The organization's role(s) in the data processing ecosystem are identified and communicated

controls.title: Priorities for organizational mission, objectives, and activities are established and communicated
controls.description: Priorities for organizational mission, objectives, and activities are established and communicated

controls.title: Systems/products/services that support organizational priorities are identified and key requirements communicated
controls.description: Systems/products/services that support organizational priorities are identified and key requirements communicated

controls.title: Contextual factors related to the systems/products/services and the data actions are identified (e.g., individuals' demographics and privacy interests or perceptions, data sensitivity and/or types, visibility of data processing to individuals and third parties)
controls.description: Contextual factors related to the systems/products/services and the data actions are identified (e.g., individuals' demographics and privacy interests or perceptions, data sensitivity and/or types, visibility of data processing to individuals and third parties)

controls.title: Data analytic inputs and outputs are identified and evaluated for bias
controls.description: Data analytic inputs and outputs are identified and evaluated for bias

controls.title: Potential problematic data actions and associated problems are identified
controls.description: Potential problematic data actions and associated problems are identified

controls.title: Problematic data actions, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine and prioritize risk
controls.description: Problematic data actions, likelihoods, and impacts are used to determine and prioritize risk

controls.title: Risk responses are identified, prioritized, and implemented
controls.description: Risk responses are identified, prioritized, and implemented

controls.title: Data processing ecosystem risk management policies, processes, and procedures are identified, established, assessed, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
controls.description: Data processing ecosystem risk management policies, processes, and procedures are identified, established, assessed, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

controls.title: Data processing ecosystem parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners, product manufacturers, application developers) are identified, prioritized, and assessed using a privacy risk assessment process
controls.description: Data processing ecosystem parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners, product manufacturers, application developers) are identified, prioritized, and assessed using a privacy risk assessment process

controls.title: Contracts with data processing ecosystem parties are used to implement appropriate measures designed to meet the objectives of an organization's privacy program
controls.description: Contracts with data processing ecosystem parties are used to implement appropriate measures designed to meet the objectives of an organization's privacy program

controls.title: Interoperability frameworks or similar multi-party approaches are used to manage data processing ecosystem privacy risks
controls.description: Interoperability frameworks or similar multi-party approaches are used to manage data processing ecosystem privacy risks

controls.title: Data processing ecosystem parties are routinely assessed using audits, test results, or other forms of evaluations to confirm they are meeting their contractual, interoperability framework, or other obligations
controls.description: Data processing ecosystem parties are routinely assessed using audits, test results, or other forms of evaluations to confirm they are meeting their contractual, interoperability framework, or other obligations

controls.title: Organizational privacy values and policies (e.g., conditions on data processing such as data uses or retention periods, individuals' prerogatives with respect to data processing) are established and communicated
controls.description: Organizational privacy values and policies (e.g., conditions on data processing such as data uses or retention periods, individuals' prerogatives with respect to data processing) are established and communicated

controls.title: Processes to instill organizational privacy values within system/product/service development and operations are established and in place
controls.description: Processes to instill organizational privacy values within system/product/service development and operations are established and in place

controls.title: Roles and responsibilities for the workforce are established with respect to privacy
controls.description: Roles and responsibilities for the workforce are established with respect to privacy

controls.title: Privacy roles and responsibilities are coordinated and aligned with third-party stakeholders (e.g., service providers, customers, partners)
controls.description: Privacy roles and responsibilities are coordinated and aligned with third-party stakeholders (e.g., service providers, customers, partners)

controls.title: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding privacy are understood and managed
controls.description: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding privacy are understood and managed

controls.title: Governance and risk management policies, processes, and procedures address privacy risks
controls.description: Governance and risk management policies, processes, and procedures address privacy risks

controls.title: Risk management processes are established, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
controls.description: Risk management processes are established, managed, and agreed to by organizational stakeholders

controls.title: Organizational risk tolerance is determined and clearly expressed
controls.description: Organizational risk tolerance is determined and clearly expressed

controls.title: The organization's determination of risk tolerance is informed by its role(s) in the data processing ecosystem
controls.description: The organization's determination of risk tolerance is informed by its role(s) in the data processing ecosystem

controls.title: The workforce is informed and trained on its roles and responsibilities
controls.description: The workforce is informed and trained on its roles and responsibilities

controls.title: Senior executives understand their roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Senior executives understand their roles and responsibilities

controls.title: Privacy personnel understand their roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Privacy personnel understand their roles and responsibilities

controls.title: Third parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners) understand their roles and responsibilities
controls.description: Third parties (e.g., service providers, customers, partners) understand their roles and responsibilities

controls.title: Privacy risk is re-evaluated on an ongoing basis and as key factors — including the organization's business environment (e.g., introduction of new technologies), governance (e.g., legal obligations, risk tolerance), data processing, and systems/products/services — change
controls.description: Privacy risk is re-evaluated on an ongoing basis and as key factors — including the organization's business environment (e.g., introduction of new technologies), governance (e.g., legal obligations, risk tolerance), data processing, and systems/products/services — change

controls.title: Privacy values, policies, and training are reviewed, and any updates are communicated
controls.description: Privacy values, policies, and training are reviewed, and any updates are communicated

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures for assessing compliance with legal requirements and privacy policies are established and in place
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures for assessing compliance with legal requirements and privacy policies are established and in place

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures for communicating progress on managing privacy risks are established and in place
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures for communicating progress on managing privacy risks are established and in place

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures are established and in place to receive, analyze, and respond to problematic data actions disclosed to the organization from internal and external sources (e.g., internal discovery, privacy researchers, professional events)
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures are established and in place to receive, analyze, and respond to problematic data actions disclosed to the organization from internal and external sources (e.g., internal discovery, privacy researchers, professional events)

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures incorporate lessons learned from problematic data actions
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures incorporate lessons learned from problematic data actions

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures for receiving, tracking, and responding to complaints, concerns, and questions from individuals about organizational privacy practices are established and in place
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures for receiving, tracking, and responding to complaints, concerns, and questions from individuals about organizational privacy practices are established and in place

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures for authorizing data processing (e.g., organizational decisions, individual consent), revoking authorizations, and maintaining authorizations are established and in place
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures for authorizing data processing (e.g., organizational decisions, individual consent), revoking authorizations, and maintaining authorizations are established and in place

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling data review, transfer, sharing or disclosure, alteration, and deletion are established and in place (e.g., to maintain data quality, manage data retention)
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling data review, transfer, sharing or disclosure, alteration, and deletion are established and in place (e.g., to maintain data quality, manage data retention)

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling individuals' data processing preferences and requests are established and in place
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures for enabling individuals' data processing preferences and requests are established and in place

controls.title: A data life cycle to manage data is aligned and implemented with the system development life cycle to manage systems
controls.description: A data life cycle to manage data is aligned and implemented with the system development life cycle to manage systems

controls.title: Data elements can be accessed for review
controls.description: Data elements can be accessed for review

controls.title: Data elements can be accessed for transmission or disclosure
controls.description: Data elements can be accessed for transmission or disclosure

controls.title: Data elements can be accessed for alteration
controls.description: Data elements can be accessed for alteration

controls.title: Data elements can be accessed for deletion
controls.description: Data elements can be accessed for deletion

controls.title: Data are destroyed according to policy
controls.description: Data are destroyed according to policy

controls.title: Data are transmitted using standardized formats
controls.description: Data are transmitted using standardized formats

controls.title: Mechanisms for transmitting processing permissions and related data values with data elements are established and in place
controls.description: Mechanisms for transmitting processing permissions and related data values with data elements are established and in place

controls.title: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed in accordance with policy and incorporating the principle of data minimization
controls.description: Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed in accordance with policy and incorporating the principle of data minimization

controls.title: Technical measures implemented to manage data processing are tested and assessed
controls.description: Technical measures implemented to manage data processing are tested and assessed

controls.title: Stakeholder privacy preferences are included in algorithmic design objectives and outputs are evaluated against these preferences
controls.description: Stakeholder privacy preferences are included in algorithmic design objectives and outputs are evaluated against these preferences

controls.title: Transparency policies, processes, and procedures for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established and in place
controls.description: Transparency policies, processes, and procedures for communicating data processing purposes, practices, and associated privacy risks are established and in place

controls.title: Roles and responsibilities for communicating with individuals are established
controls.description: Roles and responsibilities for communicating with individuals are established

controls.title: Mechanisms (e.g., notices, internal or public reports) for communicating data processing purposes, practices, associated privacy risks, and options for enabling individuals' data processing preferences and requests are established and in place
controls.description: Mechanisms (e.g., notices, internal or public reports) for communicating data processing purposes, practices, associated privacy risks, and options for enabling individuals' data processing preferences and requests are established and in place

controls.title: Mechanisms for obtaining feedback from individuals (e.g., surveys or focus groups) about data processing and associated privacy risks are established and in place
controls.description: Mechanisms for obtaining feedback from individuals (e.g., surveys or focus groups) about data processing and associated privacy risks are established and in place

controls.title: System/product/service design enables data processing visibility
controls.description: System/product/service design enables data processing visibility

controls.title: Records of data disclosures and sharing are maintained and can be reviewed
controls.description: Records of data disclosures and sharing are maintained and can be reviewed

controls.title: Data corrections or deletions can be communicated to individuals or organizations (e.g., data sources) in the data processing ecosystem
controls.description: Data corrections or deletions can be communicated to individuals or organizations (e.g., data sources) in the data processing ecosystem

controls.title: Data provenance and lineage are maintained and can be reviewed
controls.description: Data provenance and lineage are maintained and can be reviewed

controls.title: Impacted individuals and organizations are notified about a privacy breach or event
controls.description: Impacted individuals and organizations are notified about a privacy breach or event

controls.title: Individuals are provided with mitigation mechanisms (e.g., credit monitoring, consent withdrawal, data alteration or deletion) to address impacts of problematic data actions
controls.description: Individuals are provided with mitigation mechanisms (e.g., credit monitoring, consent withdrawal, data alteration or deletion) to address impacts of problematic data actions

controls.title: A baseline configuration of information technology is created and maintained incorporating security principles (e.g., concept of least functionality)
controls.description: A baseline configuration of information technology is created and maintained incorporating security principles (e.g., concept of least functionality)

controls.title: Configuration change control processes are established and in place
controls.description: Configuration change control processes are established and in place

controls.title: Backups of information are conducted, maintained, and tested
controls.description: Backups of information are conducted, maintained, and tested

controls.title: Policy and regulations regarding the physical operating environment for organizational assets are met
controls.description: Policy and regulations regarding the physical operating environment for organizational assets are met

controls.title: Protections against data leaks are implemented
controls.description: Protections against data leaks are implemented

controls.title: Data are destroyed according to policy
controls.description: Data are destroyed according to policy

controls.title: Policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to manage the protection of data
controls.description: Policies, processes, and procedures are maintained and used to manage the protection of data

controls.title: Response plans (Incident Response and Business Continuity) and recovery plans (Incident Recovery and Disaster Recovery) are established, in place, and managed
controls.description: Response plans (Incident Response and Business Continuity) and recovery plans (Incident Recovery and Disaster Recovery) are established, in place, and managed

controls.title: Response and recovery plans are tested
controls.description: Response and recovery plans are tested

controls.title: A privacy-aware software development life cycle (SDLC) including technology development practices is implemented
controls.description: A privacy-aware software development life cycle (SDLC) including technology development practices is implemented

controls.title: A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented
controls.description: A vulnerability management plan is developed and implemented

controls.title: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized individuals, processes, and devices
controls.description: Identities and credentials are issued, managed, verified, revoked, and audited for authorized individuals, processes, and devices

controls.title: Physical access to data and devices is managed
controls.description: Physical access to data and devices is managed

controls.title: Remote access is managed
controls.description: Remote access is managed

controls.title: Access permissions and authorizations are managed, incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
controls.description: Access permissions and authorizations are managed, incorporating the principles of least privilege and separation of duties

controls.title: Network integrity is protected (e.g., network segregation, network segmentation)
controls.description: Network integrity is protected (e.g., network segregation, network segmentation)

controls.title: Individuals and devices are proofed and bound to credentials, and authenticated commensurate with the risk of the transaction (e.g., individuals' security and privacy risks and other organizational risks)
controls.description: Individuals and devices are proofed and bound to credentials, and authenticated commensurate with the risk of the transaction (e.g., individuals' security and privacy risks and other organizational risks)

controls.title: Data-at-rest are protected
controls.description: Data-at-rest are protected

controls.title: Data-in-transit are protected
controls.description: Data-in-transit are protected

controls.title: Systems/products/services and associated data are formally managed throughout removal, transfers, and disposition
controls.description: Systems/products/services and associated data are formally managed throughout removal, transfers, and disposition

controls.title: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained
controls.description: Adequate capacity to ensure availability is maintained

controls.title: Protections against data leaks are implemented
controls.description: Protections against data leaks are implemented

controls.title: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software, firmware, and information integrity
controls.description: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software, firmware, and information integrity

controls.title: The development and testing environments are separate from the production environment
controls.description: The development and testing environments are separate from the production environment

controls.title: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify hardware integrity
controls.description: Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify hardware integrity

controls.title: Maintenance and repair of organizational assets are performed and logged, with approved and controlled tools
controls.description: Maintenance and repair of organizational assets are performed and logged, with approved and controlled tools

controls.title: Remote maintenance of organizational assets is approved, logged, and performed in a manner that prevents unauthorized access
controls.description: Remote maintenance of organizational assets is approved, logged, and performed in a manner that prevents unauthorized access

controls.title: Removable media is protected and its use restricted according to policy
controls.description: Removable media is protected and its use restricted according to policy

controls.title: The principle of least functionality is incorporated by configuring systems to provide only essential capabilities
controls.description: The principle of least functionality is incorporated by configuring systems to provide only essential capabilities

controls.title: Communications and control networks are protected
controls.description: Communications and control networks are protected

controls.title: Mechanisms (e.g., failsafe, load balancing, hot swap) are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations
controls.description: Mechanisms (e.g., failsafe, load balancing, hot swap) are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations
